rulings.Case Title: VERNON LEROY GIESICK et al VS. ACANDS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 24, 2008 LINE 10. DEFENDANT ELEMENTIS CHEMICALS, INC.'S, F/K/A HARCROS, AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: It does not appear that there is a basis for the Court to take judicial notice of the Persson Declaration or the Meyers' testimony for the truth of the contents of these documents. That being so, defendant has failed to shift the burden of proof. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: ONEBEACON INSURANCE COMPANY FKA GENERAL ACCIDENT VS. PANKOW RESIDENTIAL BUILDERS II, LP et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 24, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT PANKOW RESIDENTIAL BUILDERS II, L.P.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: The cross motions are denied. There are triable issues of fact that including whether the evidence presented by the parties conclusively establishes that Pankow, alone, was responsible for the injury (P8; P9 based on reliance on inadmissible the Admissions); the circumstances surrounding the tender (P17, 18 & 19); the coverage for a specific location during a specific period (P30, 32, 37); and the interplay and meaning of various parts of the policy (P38, 39, 40, 49). These factual disputes and the inferences therefrom preclude either motion from being granted. The Court's rulings on the evidence objections are as follows: Pankow's objections of Onebeacon's evidence in support - overruled objections to Lasker Declaration 1, 2, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, and 11; sustained 3, 4 and 5; Harding Declaration overruled 1 and 2; sustained 3; Roopnarine Declaration overruled 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and 6; Zirpoli Declaration 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5; and Neubauer Declaration overruled 1. Pankow objections to Onebeacon evidence in opposition overruled 1, 5, 7, 8 and 9; sustained 2, 3, 4, and 6; and plaintiff's objections to evidence in defendant's opposition overruled 3; sustained 1 and 2. Judicial notice is taken of the Register of Actions in case 02-403697. =(302/PJM/vc)

rulings.Case Title: YOSHABEL CLEMENTS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Order For Dismissal Of Complaint With Prejudice
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 24, 2008 LINE 4. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND DENNIS HERRERA'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Order For Dismissal Of Complaint With Prejudice: HEARING REQUIRED TO ADDRESS WHY THIS MOTION SHOULD/OR SHOULD NOT BE SENT TO JUDGE DONDERO FOR DECISION GIVEN THAT HE HAS RETAINED JURISDICTION OVER THE PROPERTY. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: DAN MESCHER VS. PACIFIC,GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 24, 2008 LINE 5. DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMMUNICATIONS ARE PRIVILEGED AND PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ALLEGE MALICE. =(302/PJM/X-MS)

rulings.Case Title: MAURA P. LOREDO VS. ALL AMERICAN FINANCIAL GROUP INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Transfer Action To Proper Court
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 24, 2008 LINE 6. DEFENDANTS ALL AMERICAN FINANCIAL GROUP INC., BABAK BROUKHIM AND EUGENE TYLER'S MOTION TO Transfer Action To Proper Court IS DENIED. THE COMPLAINT, COUNTS NINE AND TEN ARE PREMISED ON THE EXISTENCE OF A CONTRACT. C.C.P. SECTION 395(B) PROVIDES A BASIS FOR VENUE. PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO REASONABLE FEES UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 396(B). =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT M BASHARA SR VS. MARK CASILLAS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 24, 2008 LINE 7. DEFENDANT MARK CASILLAS' DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. DEFENDANT DOES NOT ADDRESS THE ALLEGATIONS RELATED TO DELAWARE CODE SECTION 225. A GENERAL DEMURRER DOES NOT LIE TO ONLY A PART OF A CAUSE OF ACTION. FINANCIAL CORP. V WILBURN (1987) 189 CAL APP 3D 764,778. =(302/PJM/AY)
SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 24, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANT PETER TRIPOUSIS' DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR PER MOVING PARTY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JACKSON PLAZA HOMEOWNERS VS MADJA CORP
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 24, 2008 LINE 9. DEFENDANTS GLORIA TAI, TAI ASSOCIATES AND VINCENT TAI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: The decision in Lantzy v. Centex Homes (2003) 31 Cal.4th 363 is a change in the law related to CCP section 337.15. To the extent the motion relates to CCP section 337.15, the Court may consider it if due diligence has been shown. Baldwin v. Home Savings of America (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 1192, 199-1200. The affidavit filed by defendants fails to explain the delay in bringing this motion. To the extent that defendants rely on different subsections of statutes previously relied upon is not a basis to consider the motion as explained in Baldwin. The Court also notes that there is no accepted legal definition of the term "developer" and given defendants various roles, there is no set of undisputed facts from which one could make such a determination or from which one could conclude, as a matter of law, that the defendants were not in "actual possession or the control, as owner, tenant or otherwise" of the subject premises. Given plaintiffs position in response to the motion, the Court grants the motion as to the 5th, 16th and 17th issues. The motion is denied as to all other issues. =(302/PJM/PB)
